decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares enrollment, demographics, and microbiology across regions to assess whether geographic recruitment shifts threaten trial generalizability.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Directly evaluates the generalizability of antibacterial trial populations across geographic regions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generalizability appears only as a geographic limitation of the SLATE II trial; no generalizability method is used or evaluated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A treatment-algorithm trial, not a lineage source for generalizability methods.
named_source_mentions: Simplified Algorithm for Treatment Eligibility (SLATE) I trial

decisions.relationship_evidence: The cohort methodology is evaluated for enrollment bias, while generalizability is only a desired property of future data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Develops technology-mediated cohort recruitment rather than a transportability or generalizability method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses global sequencing data to test whether Oxfordshire serotype findings generalize across geographically diverse isolates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides an explicit cross-population assessment of finding generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends transportability to trial subgroup analyses and illustrates target-specific subgroup effects and numbers needed to treat.
decisions.lineage_evidence: An explicitly methodological source developing a new use of the transportability framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review says weak study designs limit generalizability and translation but does not use or evaluate a generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is only an evidence-quality concern in a digital-health review.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses inverse-odds weighting and imputation to transport efavirenz trial effects to an HIV clinical-care target population.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of trial transportability to HIV treatment safety.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Discusses social factors limiting model generalizability but does not present, apply, or evaluate a generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Conceptual infectious-disease modeling discussion rather than a generalizability-method source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generalizability is mentioned only as a limitation arising from oral-polio-vaccine coadministration patterns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A rotavirus efficacy trial without a transportability or generalizability analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Externally tests identical COVID-19 CT classifiers across country-specific datasets and documents severe performance loss under data shift.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit empirical evaluation of model generalizability across external populations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uncertain generalizability is reported only as a limitation of the Malawi cohort study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No transportability or generalizability method is applied or assessed.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Shows that sensitivity estimates may generalize poorly to target populations and thereby bias corrected seroprevalence estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Directly diagnoses a target-population generalizability problem in serosurveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Voluntary participation is said to limit generalizability, but the serosurvey does not perform a generalizability analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is only a study limitation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Trial conditions are described as limiting generalizability to higher-transmission settings; no transport method is used.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A chemoprevention trial with a generalizability caveat only.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs independent external validation and quantifies reduced COVID-19 imaging-model performance relative to internal testing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly assesses model generalizability and reproducibility using external multicenter data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generalizability to other settings is only identified as uncertain because the trial used one urban clinic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No generalizability method or external-population evaluation is reported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generalizability beyond school populations and settings is mentioned only as a limitation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A nested menstrual-cup trial without a generalizability analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Systematically reviews representativeness and external validity of bloodstream-infection trials and identifies underrepresented groups and barriers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A focused evidence review evaluating trial-population generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses bacterial-infection risk-model transportability across tertiary and community ICU datasets, including external validation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct empirical transportability assessment of a clinical prediction model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes statistical–simulation model synthesis with g-computation and inverse-probability estimators for positivity violations in transportability.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development addressing a core transportability assumption.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Quantifies predictive-model generalizability and compares performance before and after inverse-probability weighting for catchment selection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Evaluates external validity and weighting effects for C. difficile prediction models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates infectious-disease prediction models on national and regional external datasets to assess their generalizability.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides explicit external-data evaluation of model generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper requests future multicenter confirmation of generalizability but does not itself conduct such an analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A single-center treatment-delivery trial with a generalizability caveat.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Potential lack of generalizability beyond participating IeDEA sites is stated only as a limitation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No transportability or generalizability method is used.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Externally validates England-trained AMR models in Uganda, Nigeria, and Tanzania to assess cross-country applicability and performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct multi-country evaluation of prediction-model generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Lack of monitoring outside Niger is identified only as limiting generalizability.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The resistance substudy does not perform transport or external generalizability analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares marginal standardization and raking scenarios for generalizing Montréal pediatric seroprevalence to the census-defined target population.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparison of weighting methods used to assess population generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops scPAGE2 transfer learning to improve diagnostic-model generalizability across transcriptomic platforms and validates it on seven datasets.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops and validates a strategy for improving model generalizability.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The digital-twin survey discusses generalizability among several validation issues but does not present or evaluate a generalizability method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Generalizability is a review dimension, not the paper's methodological subject.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tests MSKI-RADS reliability and accuracy across 22 readers from nine institutions as a wider generalizability study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit broader validation of a diagnostic classification system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Transportability analysis is listed as one component of immunobridging integration, without applying, developing, or directly evaluating it.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract does not provide a substantive transportability-method contribution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates MALDI-TOF classifiers across platforms and attributes limited generalizability to regional and phylogenetic strain heterogeneity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Directly evaluates cross-platform model performance and its generalizability limits.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies nested raking to align residual-serum observations with the US age–sex population distribution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HCV-prevalence application of population weighting for generalization.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generalizability is mentioned only as potentially limited by serosurvey design and measurement.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No transportability or generalizability procedure is reported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses matching-adjusted indirect comparison to reweight Chinese real-world cohorts against global trial arms and assess local extrapolation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-treatment application of trial-to-local-population reweighting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Shows that strain-specific treatment versions require additional transportability considerations beyond those for noncompound exposures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: An explicitly methodological causal-inference analysis of transportability under pathogen-strain heterogeneity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses machine-learning transportability models to generalize a Senegal malaria trial to 116 nontrial communes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of trial transportability to environmentally mediated malaria intervention effects.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrates human, animal, outbreak, travel, food-production, and trade datasets into a standardized Salmonella source-attribution model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete cross-system synthesis of multiple surveillance and monitoring sources.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines sentinel consultations, virologic sampling, and laboratory pathogen reports in a Bayesian evidence synthesis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete cross-system synthesis for estimating influenza among influenza-like illness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Triangulates vaccination records, primary-care records, susceptibility estimates, and measles-incidence surveillance to identify immunity gaps.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete routine-data triangulation for measles program assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Triangulates two population health surveys with a national laboratory sentinel system to characterize chlamydia epidemiology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete cross-system triangulation of survey and laboratory data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Triangulates national meningitis surveillance with prospective cohort datasets to adjust missing cryptococcal-meningitis data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete use of cross-system triangulation in national HIV-program monitoring.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses mixed-method benchmarking against HepCare, but the abstract does not describe cross-system triangulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: An intervention evaluation rather than a triangulation-method source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a data-triangulation framework comparing cohort, surveillance, ART-sales, and literature sources to estimate cohort coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework for cross-system cohort-coverage triangulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts a qualitative literature synthesis specifically examining how different forms of trust affect COVID-19 vaccine acceptance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete trust-focused vaccine-acceptance review.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews historical and contemporary evidence on AI and machine-learning approaches for influenza epidemic and pandemic forecasting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete AI/ML infectious-disease forecasting review.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks daily WHONET-SaTScan cluster detection against rule-based infection-control clusters and expert assessments.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of automated hospital-outbreak detection performance.
supported_label_variants: Automated detection of infectious disease outbreaks

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses SaTScan and WHONet-SaTScan to detect bacterial-species and resistance outbreaks in adjacent ICUs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of statistical outbreak-detection software.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a space-time scan statistic to surveillance and laboratory data to identify domestic typhoid clusters and outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete algorithmic outbreak-detection application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports daily prospective space-time permutation scanning for 35 diseases that produced early outbreak alerts.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Operational application of algorithmic early-outbreak surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes an ontology-curated Twitter framework combining XGBoost and BiLSTM for outbreak detection and tracking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and experimental evaluation of a hybrid outbreak-detection method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces integration of machine-learning diagnostic predictions with spatiotemporal surveillance for earlier outbreak detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for AI-enhanced biosurveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an automated real-time surveillance system combining several algorithms for HIV and hepatitis outbreak-state detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit system and method development with sensitivity, specificity, and timeliness evaluation.
supported_label_variants: automated surveillance system for outbreak detection

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a machine-learning framework using MALDI-TOF spectra, resistance patterns, and their fusion for hospital-outbreak detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a rapid alternative outbreak-detection framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates the effective reproduction number during the initial Los Lagos H1N1 outbreak phase.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak application of reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates the initial reproduction number for the 2009 H1N1 epidemic wave on Réunion Island.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete influenza application of reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a new modeling method that accounts for imported cases when estimating temporal effective reproduction numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for reproduction-number estimation under importation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends prospective effective-reproduction-number estimation to accommodate reporting delays and repeated importations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension for real-time transmissibility surveillance.
supported_label_variants: Real-time monitoring of the effective reproduction number

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses three complementary methods to estimate spatial and temporal reproduction-number variation during Thailand's H5N1 epidemic.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete avian-influenza application of reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes Bayesian stochastic SIR models using notifications and importation data for real-time effective-reproduction-number estimation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model development for real-time reproduction-number monitoring.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends the Wallinga–Teunis algorithm with spatial information to estimate Rift Valley fever transmissibility over time.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit spatial extension of a reproduction-number estimation algorithm.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares three routine-surveillance methods for estimating measles R and assesses consistency as evidence of method validity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative evaluation of reproduction-number estimators.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Employs novel piecewise exponential methodology for real-time estimation of changing Ebola transmission and effective reproduction numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological contribution to real-time transmissibility assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits an SEIR model to West African Ebola data to estimate basic and effective reproduction numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Ebola application of reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Computes SARS-CoV-2 Rt across 125 countries and analyzes its association with mobility indicators.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Large-scale applied transmissibility analysis using effective reproduction numbers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a GPS-based social-contact model that directly calculates the effective reproduction number by activity and location.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development linking mobility-derived contacts to reproduction numbers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates district-level SARS-CoV-2 Rt and models its association with weather, mobility, policy, and infrastructure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete spatiotemporal reproduction-number surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a mobility-adjusted effective-reproduction-number metric for evaluating SARS-CoV-2 variant infectivity and forecasting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a reproduction-number adjustment metric.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and simulates a Bayesian model-selection framework for location- and time-specific reproduction-number estimation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit generic methodology for spatiotemporal transmissibility surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Models temperature and humidity effects on prefecture-specific time-varying effective reproductive numbers in Japan.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 reproduction-number application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian framework and national registry data to estimate pulmonary-tuberculosis time-varying reproduction numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete registry-based reproduction-number surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents an early Rt indicator from digital contact data with an innovative decomposition into contact and infection probabilities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Develops a real-time, decomposable reproduction-number surveillance indicator.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculates the basic reproduction number while characterizing transmission chains in Uganda's 2022 Sudan-virus outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Ebola-outbreak application of reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses EpiEstim with parametric and nonparametric serial-interval approaches to estimate time-varying Re for a COVID-19 outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete outbreak-response application of effective-reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates COVID-19 basic reproduction numbers, doubling times, and daily growth rates across five Iranian provinces.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete regional transmissibility estimation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses EpiEstim on sentinel surveillance data to estimate Omicron effective reproduction numbers in Guinea.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete post-epidemic transmissibility-surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses deterministic and stochastic models to estimate national and island-specific farm-level ASF reproduction numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete animal-outbreak application of reproduction-number estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian network to model climate and farm-management associations with tick presence on Portuguese pig farms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vector-risk application of a Bayesian network model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a Bayesian belief network to estimate regional whirling-disease risk in native trout populations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease risk-model application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a Bayesian belief-network model quantifying Ebola exposure risk to wastewater workers under multiple scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A newly developed but domain-specific infectious-risk application of the model class.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes and demonstrates a methodology for co-designing, quantifying, and validating expert-elicited nonparametric Bayesian networks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and guidance for Bayesian-network risk modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an interpretable ISM-K2 Bayesian-network model combining expert judgment and historical vaccination data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a new Bayesian-network structure and evaluation method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian networks to analyze direct and indirect factors associated with HPV-vaccination intention.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vaccine-behavior application of Bayesian-network modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates CUSUM, structural, and Bayesian change-point methods on surveillance and simulated data, including autocorrelation sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of change-point-analysis performance for outbreak surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes Bayesian bivariate change-point models that estimate correlation between change points in two longitudinal outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of multivariate change-point models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a two-stage maximum-likelihood change-point model for estimating assay limits of detection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and simulation assessment of a change-point estimator.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a hierarchical Bayesian change-point model to estimate local onset timing of RSV epidemics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of change-point modeling to RSV prophylaxis scheduling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks four change-point methods against expert inspection on 840 simulated outbreak time series.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation and recommendation among change-point algorithms.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an exact unconditional change-point test for binary sequences and demonstrates improved small-sample power.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit statistical-method development for change-point detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses change-point methods to identify age- and cohort-specific declines in HPV-associated cervical lesions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vaccine-impact application of change-point analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines Bayesian model averaging with change-point models to estimate intervention-associated timing and magnitude changes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for vaccine-impact change detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses change-point analysis to locate plateaus in CD4 count, percentage, and CD4/CD8 recovery during ART.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete longitudinal HIV application of change-point analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies change-point detection to longitudinal microbiome abundances and clinical-state changes during cystic-fibrosis exacerbations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete microbiome and pulmonary-infection application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a changepoint-assisted periodic ARMA modeling and selection algorithm for infectious-disease incidence time series.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development using changepoints to identify extreme incidence periods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses change-point analysis to independently identify step changes in seizure admissions following rotavirus-vaccine introduction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vaccine-impact application of change-point analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a change-point model to surveillance data to detect a postvaccination decline in EV71 disease trends.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete early vaccine-impact application of change-point modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a semiparametric respiratory-illness model incorporating a change point and growth curve for intervention effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model development using a change-point component to isolate human-intervention effects.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Advances self-normalized change-point testing and combines it with NOT for multiple-change-point estimation and forecasting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of single and multiple change-point procedures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an exact segmentation-based change-point test with controlled type-I error and improved small-sample power.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit statistical-method development for exact change-point detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies change-point detection to Belgian excess-death data to separate pre- and post-COVID-19 periods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pandemic-mortality application of change-point detection.
